theme: Nova API versioning breaking change deployed to production affecting 6 integrations plus Vertex Series A data room preparation deadline

events.text: CRITICAL: API v2 deployed at 09:55 with breaking change — field renamed user_id to account_id, 6 active integrations returning 500s
NOVA-1201 Critical — v2 breaking change: user_id renamed to account_id, 6 integrations broken, deployed 09:55
Infra: all 6 affected integrations sending requests with user_id field — receiving 422 Unprocessable Entity since 09:55
REVERT: api-schema-v2.py — field rename not backwards-compatible, breaking all v1 integration clients
Engineering: revert too slow — 6 customers affected now, faster to add backwards compatibility shim for user_id
fix: add backwards compatibility shim — accept both user_id and account_id, map user_id to account_id internally
NOVA-1202 — backwards compatibility shim deploying, accepts user_id and account_id both
Shim deployed at 10:02 — testing against all 6 affected integrations
All 6 integrations returning 200 — shim working, 7-minute breaking change window closed
NOVA-1201 resolved — all 6 integrations restored at 10:02, 7-minute impact
PM: notifying all 6 affected integration partners — explaining field rename, deprecation timeline for user_id
Subject: API v2 field change — user_id to account_id | Body: Temporary shim active, user_id deprecated but still accepted, migration guide attached, 90-day sunset
NOVA-1203 — 90-day deprecation notice sent to 6 integration partners for user_id field
Engineering: auditing why breaking change reached production — versioning policy requires backward-compat in minor releases
test: audit deployment pipeline — identify how field rename bypassed backward-compat check
